# Description Fixes #1480 This Pull Request resolves a bug where the **Project Statistics Dashboard** displays all zeroes (`0`) and the project grid fails to load correctly on the front-end page. ## Bug Analysis During the iteration over `projectsData` in `web-app/index.html`, the code attempted to access `proj.difficulty.toLowerCase()`. Since `difficulty` was only defined for one project in `projectsData`, processing the second project (`2048-game`) threw a `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toLowerCase')`. Because this error occurred within the immediate script block, it halted the execution of the rest of the script, preventing the dashboard statistics counters from updating and animating. ## Proposed Changes - **Defensively Render Difficulty Badges**: Updated `web-app/index.html` (around line 1201) to conditionally check if `proj.difficulty` is defined before rendering the difficulty badge HTML and attempting to call `.toLowerCase()`. ```javascript card.innerHTML = ` ${proj.difficulty ? `
${proj.difficulty}
` : ''} ...

items.title: feat: support touch events for mobile drag and drop in opening trainer
items.body:
# Description

This PR implements full mobile/tablet touch support for the Chess Opening Trainer board, allowing mobile users to drag and drop pieces smoothly to make moves.

Closes #2168

## Proposed Changes

### JavaScript: `opening_trainer.js`
* Added global variables to keep track of active touch dragging state (`activeTouchImg`, `touchOffsetX`, `touchOffsetY`).
* Implemented new touch handlers:
  * `handleTouchStart`: Captures touch drag, locks image coordinates, handles offset calculations, and changes image display style to `fixed` and `pointer-events: none`.
  * `handleTouchMove`: Updates floating piece image location based on the active touch coordinate.
  * `handleTouchEnd`: Detects the targeted chess square using `document.elementFromPoint`, triggers the chess move logic via `makeUserMove` (if the target square is different from the origin square), and cleans up styles by calling `renderBoard()`.
* Bound `touchstart`, `touchmove`, `touchend`, and `touchcancel` events to the chess pieces in `renderBoard()`.

### CSS: `opening_trainer.css`
* Applied `touch-action: none` to the `.square img` chess piece elements to prevent default page panning/scrolling while dragging pieces on touchscreens.

# Description

Implements a **Move Hint System** that highlights the correct piece and destination square on the board when the user requests help during opening training.

Fixes #2167

- Assists users when they get stuck on complex opening theory.
- Provides a clean, aesthetic visual cue on the board without interrupting the general flow.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Template
- **Modified:** `game/templates/game/opening_detail.html`
  - Added a "Get Hint" button next to the input and check move button.

  - Styled the new hint button (`#get-hint-btn`) to fit seamlessly with the board theme.
  - Added keyframes and styling for `.square.hint-from` to show a subtle yellow pulsing glow on the starting square of the correct piece.
  - Added styling for `.square.hint-to` to display a dashed yellow border highlighting the target square.

### 3. JavaScript Logic
- **Modified:** `game/static/game/js/opening_trainer.js`
  - Introduced `hintHighlight` and `hintButton` state handlers.
  - Added an event listener to the "Get Hint" button that parses the current expected move using `parseSAN` and marks the relevant coordinates.
  - Updated `renderBoard()` to apply the `hint-from` and `hint-to` styling to the board.
  - Configured state resets so that the hint highlight automatically disappears as soon as a user clicks another square, resets the board, moves historical steps, or makes a move.
  - Updated `updateInputState` to disable the hint button when the opening trainer is completed or when viewing historical steps.

items.title: feat: add interactive move navigation stepper and reset buttons
items.body:
# Description

Fixes #2165 

This PR implements interactive move navigation controls below the chess board in the Opening Trainer (resolving #2165). This allows users to navigate back and forth through the history of completed moves to review their progress at their own pace.

## Key Additions & Changes

### 1. UI Components (`opening_detail.html`)
- Added a new navigation control bar wrapper containing `← Previous`, `Reset`, and `Next →` buttons directly underneath the chess board.

### 2. Glassmorphic Styling (`opening_trainer.css`)
- Implemented a sleek glassmorphic card container with translucent backgrounds, high-quality backdrop filters, and subtle shadows.
- Styled custom buttons with states (hover transitions, active clicks, and clear `disabled` layouts) matching the project's warm dark gold (`#f0c040`) color palette.

### 3. Navigation & Input Locking Logic (`opening_trainer.js`)
- Introduced a `viewingMoveIndex` state to keep track of the current move index visible on the board, independently of the furthest progress index (`currentMove`).
- Added `showMoveAt(index)` to dynamically rebuild the `boardState` from any historical point in the move array and update the visual board highlights.
- Prevented piece selection, drag events, and manual text move checks whenever the user is viewing historical moves (i.e. when `viewingMoveIndex < currentMove`).
- Enabled Left Arrow (`ArrowLeft`) and Right Arrow (`ArrowRight`) keyboard listeners for quick, intuitive navigation.
- Linked the `Reset` button to trigger the existing clean game reset behavior.

items.title: feat: implement smooth theme switch transitions and toast notifications
items.body:
### Description

This Pull Request resolves #2083 by introducing smooth CSS transitions when toggling between dark and light modes, and triggering a toast notification indicating the newly active theme mode.

---

### Goals & Solution

1. **Smooth CSS Transitions:**
   - Added transition properties (`background-color`, `color`, `border-color`, and `box-shadow` over `0.3s ease`) to global structural selectors (body, navbar, cards, forms, buttons, dropdowns, headers, and text elements) in `theme.css`.
   
2. **Toast Notifications on Theme Toggle:**
   - Modified `theme.js` to call the toast component when toggling the theme.
   - Implemented **on-demand dynamic loading** of the toast system (`toast.js` & `toast.css`) inside `theme.js`. This guarantees that theme switches trigger toast notifications even on pages where the toast assets are not statically loaded (like the landing/homepage), without bloating initial page load times.

3. **No Page Flash / Load Flash Prevention:**
   - Moved the initial theme detection and `setAttribute` operations to the top-level scope of `theme.js` (outside `DOMContentLoaded`). Because `theme.js` is loaded synchronously in the `<head>` of HTML templates, setting the attribute early ensures the page is parsed and rendered with the correct theme on the very first paint. This eliminates layout flash and prevents transitions from triggering on page load/refresh.

---
Fixes #2083

items.title: feat: Port Pygame Chess to Web-App dashboard
items.body:
# Description

This Pull Request ports the local Python Pygame Chess game to the interactive HTML/CSS/JavaScript web application dashboard. It implements a fully responsive chess board with standard move validation, board theme customization, turn status indicators, checkmate/stalemate indicators, and support for both Pass & Play and a Minimax AI player.

Closes #1283

## Key Features

1. **Chess Engine & Game Logic**:
   - Implements full chess rules including standard piece movements, castling, en passant, pawn promotion selection (Queen, Rook, Bishop, Knight), and checkmate/stalemate validation.
   - Designed with clean, circular-dependency-free validation, separating attack maps from castling safety checks to prevent stack overflow errors.
2. **Premium Board UI**:
   - Built a clean 8x8 grid utilizing CSS Grid.
   - Styled chess pieces using solid Unicode vectors colored with CSS (`#ffffff` for White, `#1c2024` for Black) and drop-shadows to ensure high contrast and visibility against all light/dark squares.
   - Highlight overlays for active piece selection and valid legal destinations.
3. **Theme Customization**:
   - Supported board theme switcher: Classic Forest (Green/Cream), Warm Wood (Brown/Tan), and Charcoal (Dark/Light Gray).
4. **Game Modes**:
   - Local Two-Player Pass & Play mode.
   - Single Player vs. Computer AI mode (one-level lookahead Minimax evaluation engine).

## Changes List

- **[NEW]** `web-app/js/projects/chess.js`: Contains UI markup (`getChessHTML`), board CSS styles, move generator, minimax AI search logic, and UI bindings (`initChess`).
- **[MODIFY]** `projects_registry.json`: Appended metadata entry for "Chess Game" to register it with the main codebase.
- **[MODIFY]** `web-app/js/projects.js`: Registered chess initialization handler and added steps to instructions tooltip map.
- **[MODIFY]** `web-app/games.html`: Added Chess project card on the dashboard and linked the script source file.

## Verification Checklist

- [x] Board loads and renders correctly without any console errors or stack overflows.
- [x] Selecting a piece highlights all valid legal destinations.
- [x] Making a move switches the turn and active indicators.
- [x] Castling and pawn promotion work as expected.
- [x] Switching themes updates the board colors seamlessly.
- [x] Playing against the AI triggers automatic computer moves on Black's turn.

## Description

Fixes #6222

Implements a lightweight, edge-compatible SVG optimization and minification utility to reduce initial SVG payload sizes by 15–30%+ on Vercel Edge Runtime. 

Key changes include:
- Created a state-of-the-art edge-compatible optimizer in `lib/svg/optimizer.ts` that isolates text segments and CSS rules during optimization to preserve user text spacing, font styling, animations, and transitions exactly.
- Added support for SVG comment removal, coordinate float precision rounding (to 2 decimal places), path data formatting, and whitespace compression.
- Added a `minify=true` parameter (on by default) to the validations schema in `lib/validations.ts` and integrated it directly inside `app/api/streak/route.ts`.
- Documented the new `minify` parameter in `docs/customization.md`.
- Added comprehensive unit tests in `lib/svg/optimizer.test.ts` and integration tests in `app/api/streak/route.test.ts`.

- [ ] 🕐 Pillar 3 — Timezone Logic Optimization

This PR refactors `game/analysis.py` to replace a standard `print()` statement with Django's logging configuration, resolving the existing `# TODO: replace with project logger` comment on line 13.

- Instantiated the logger using `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)`.
- Replaced the `print(f"warning: opening book not found at {OPENINGS_JSON_PATH}")` statement with:
  ```python
  logger.warning("Opening book not found at %s", OPENINGS_JSON_PATH)
- Removed the `# TODO: replace with project logger` comment.

### Related Issue
Closes #1950

items.body:
## Description

Fixes #6221

This Pull Request introduces the **Square Root (`sqrt`) scaling** model for 3D isometric tower heights and weekly skyline structures. 

Currently, users have two scaling options:
- **Linear scaling (`scale=linear`)**: Causes high-contribution days to overwhelm low-contribution days visually.
- **Logarithmic scaling (`scale=log`)**: Compresses variance so much that differences between low-to-medium days are barely noticeable.

A **Square Root (`sqrt`) scaling** model acts as the perfect middle ground, highlighting variances in low-to-medium commits while keeping high-volume days from completely dominating the grid.

### Formula
$$h_{\text{scaled}} = h_{\text{max}} \times \sqrt{\frac{\text{commits}}{\text{maxCommits}}}$$
Where $h_{\text{max}}$ is `50` (matching maximum linear and log height thresholds).

---

items.title: feat: Chess Puzzle Browser & Difficulty-based Filtering
items.body:
Fixes #1949 

## Description
Currently, chess puzzles are stored in the `ChessPuzzle` database model but are only served as a single daily puzzle. This PR implements a **Puzzle Dashboard** where users can browse, search, and filter puzzles by their difficulty (`easy`, `medium`, `hard`) and play them directly on the game board.

Additionally, to prevent users from cheating by inspecting the payload, the solution array has been excluded from the initial listing and daily puzzle endpoints, and is only retrieved on demand when the player actively starts the puzzle.

## Proposed Changes
### 1. Backend APIs & Security (`game/views.py`, `game/urls.py`)
- Created `puzzles_view` to render the dashboard template.
- Added RESTful endpoints:
  - `GET /api/puzzles/`: Lists all puzzles without solutions. Supports `difficulty` filtering and `search` query parameters.
  - `GET /api/puzzles/<id>/`: Details of a specific puzzle (excludes solution).
  - `GET /api/puzzles/<id>/solution/`: Retrieves the solution array securely.
- Modified `get_daily_puzzle` to exclude the solution array from the initial payload.

### 2. Frontend Board Logic (`board.js`)
- Integrated query parameter handling in `loadGame()` so that navigating to `/play/?puzzle_id=<id>` automatically fetches details and solution, starting the game in AI puzzle mode and bypassing the default menu overlay.
- Updated `startDailyPuzzle()` to dynamically fetch the solution on demand.
- Fixed `restartPuzzleBtn.onclick` to correctly restart any puzzle in AI puzzle play mode instead of PvP.

### 3. Dashboard UI (`puzzle_list.html`)
- Built a premium glassmorphic dark-theme UI matching Checkora's aesthetics.
- Added dynamic filtering buttons (`All`, `Easy`, `Medium`, `Hard`) and search input that perform asynchronous server-side fetching.
- Clicking **Solve** redirects to the board with the puzzle preloaded.

### 4. Navigation Links (`landing.html`, `board.html`)
- Linked the new Puzzle Dashboard in the profile dropdowns and footer.

### 5. Unit Tests (`tests.py`)
- Created 7 test cases in `ChessPuzzleDashboardTests` verifying endpoint functionality, query parameters, detail retrieval, solution retrieval, and solution exclusion.
- Adjusted pre-existing `test_daily_puzzle_api_selects_by_date` to assert solution exclusion.

items.title: fix: Ensure Number Sliding Puzzle is solvable (#1158)
items.body:
# Description
This PR resolves #1158 by ensuring that the generated configurations for the CLI **Number Sliding Puzzle (8-puzzle)** game are mathematically solvable. 

Historically, `random.shuffle()` was used to shuffle the tiles, resulting in unsolvable board configurations 50% of the time. This PR implements an inversion-count check to guarantee that the game is always winnable.

### Mathematical Context
For a $3\times3$ grid (8-puzzle), a configuration is solvable if and only if the number of inversions is **even** (excluding the empty tile `0`).
- **Inversion:** Any pair of tiles $(a, b)$ such that $a > b$ and $a$ appears before $b$ in the sequence.

---

## Changes Proposed
1. **Added `is_solvable(numbers)` helper:** 
   - Dynamically filters out the blank tile (`0`).
   - Counts inversions using nested loops and checks if the count is even.
2. **Updated generation loop in `main()`:**
   - Shuffles the tiles repeatedly until a solvable configuration is found.
   - Ensures the board does not initialize in the already-solved win state (`[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 0]`) to maintain gameplay engagement.

---

Closes #1158

items.body:
# Description

Resolves #7956

This PR introduces visual screenshot previews (thumbnails) to all project cards on the homepage grid. Previously, the grid only displayed text metadata (title, description, tags), which made browsing less engaging. Adding visual thumbnails provides immediate visual context for each project, making the browsing experience significantly more premium, dynamic, and intuitive.

## Key Changes

### 1. Centralized Preview Generation (`scripts/generate-previews.js`)
- Configured the Puppeteer viewport to `800x500` (retaining the standard 16:10 / 1.6 aspect ratio for thumbnails) to ensure text and components are clearly readable.
- Centrally stored generated screenshots inside the `public/previews/` directory (e.g. `public/previews/<project_folder_name>.png`) instead of scattering them in individual project directories.
- Filtered out utility folders like `previews`, `images`, and `node_modules` from screenshot generation.

### 2. Auto-Mapped Metadata & Validation Fixes (`projects.json`)
- Mapped all 229 projects in the registry to their local directory paths and appended the `previewImage` property.
- Fixed 4 pre-existing path errors in the registry (specifically for *Zomato-clone*, *Quiz App Timer*, *Gemini Clone*, and *Mind Reader*) to ensure that the repository's automatic metadata schema checks pass perfectly.

### 3. Dynamic Card Rendering (`index.js`)
- Extracted and resolved the `previewImage` field relative to the current URL base, maintaining compatibility for both the main homepage and the `/contributors/` directories.
- Rendered the thumbnail inside card structures with an `onerror="this.parentNode.style.display='none';"` fallback so that cards gracefully revert to their clean text-only layout if their screenshot is missing.

### 4. Styled Container & Smooth Hover Animation (`style.css`)
- Styled `.card-preview-image-container` and its inner image to enforce rounded corners, border accents, and a dark background mask.
- Added a zoom micro-animation on hover (`scale(1.05)`) with ease transitions to make cards feel highly responsive and interactive.

items.body:
# Description

Closes #7955.

This PR implements the **"Surprise Me" / "Random Project"** feature on the homepage. With over 220+ projects in the archive, users can feel overwhelmed choosing what to build next. Having convenient entry points to pick and view a random project encourages discovery and interaction.

## Key Features

1. **Dual Entry Points**: Added a "Surprise Me" button in two strategic places:
   - **Hero Section**: Next to the primary "Browse Projects" CTA.
   - **Search/Filter Toolbar**: Next to the "Filter by Tech Stack" dropdown.
2. **Smooth Scroll & Custom Highlight**: When clicked, a random project is selected, the page automatically navigates to its paginated page, scrolls smoothly to position the card in the center of the viewport, and triggers a temporary glow/outline pulse animation to draw attention.
3. **Silent Filter Reset**: If filters are active when the button is clicked, they are programmatically cleared (active category reset to "All") silently without duplicate or redundant toast popups, ensuring the random card can always render in the main grid.

---

## Technical Details

### 1. Structure (`index.html`)
- Added `#heroSurpriseBtn` in the `.hero-actions` container.
- Added `#searchSurpriseBtn` next to the tech stack filter select element in the `.filter-toolbar` container.

### 2. Styling (`style.css`)
- Added `.project-card.highlighted` matching the theme configurations:
  - Scaled up slightly (`scale(1.03)`) for emphasis.
  - Applied a custom `outline` and neon-glowing `box-shadow` matching the accent color variables (`--accent`, `--bg-surface`, etc.) before fading back to the normal state smoothly.

### 3. Logic (`index.js`)
- Configured a unique `data-day` attribute for each project card rendered in the main grid.
- Extended `resetAllFilters()` with an optional `silent` flag to enable programmatically resetting filter elements without triggering standard user-facing toasts.
- Implemented `scrollToAndHighlightProject(project)` to find the index, paginate the grid to the correct page, render, scroll, and add the temporary highlight class.
- Bound both new buttons to `handleSurpriseMe()`.

fix: Standardize projectType values in projects.json
items.body:
## Description
The `projectType` field in `projects.json` has had inconsistent casing, naming conventions, and double categories (e.g., `tool` vs `Tool`, `game` vs `Game`, `Web Application` / `Web App` vs existing allowed categories), causing duplicate segments to appear on the Category Breakdown doughnut chart on the Stats page.

This PR standardizes `projectType` values across all 226 projects to follow a consistent title-cased vocabulary, updates the JSON Schema to enforce it, and updates the project metadata validation script to check for invalid types.

## Related Issues
Closes #7837

## Proposed Changes
1. **`projects.json`**:
   - Normalized 16 entries with non-standardized `projectType` values (e.g., `tool` -> `Tool`, `game` -> `Game`, `Web Application`/`Web App` -> `Tool`/`API`/`AI`/`Simulation`/`Animation`, `UI / Animation` -> `UI`).
2. **`schema/projects.schema.json`**:
   - Added an `enum` constraint to `projectType` specifying the list of allowed categories.
3. **`scripts/validate-metadata.js`**:
   - Added `projectType` to `REQUIRED_KEYS`.
   - Implemented validation checking to verify `projectType` is a string and belongs to the allowed set: `Game`, `Tool`, `Clone`, `UI`, `Animation`, `API`, `Website`, `Backend`, `AI`, `E-Commerce`, `Productivity`, `Simulation`, `Authentication`, `Form`.
4. **`scripts/validate-projects-fixture.js`**:
   - Updated mock project fixtures to contain the required `projectType` property.
   - Added a test case featuring an invalid `projectType` to ensure the validation script correctly identifies and catches incorrect categories.

## How Has This Been Tested?
1. **Automated Tests**:
   - Ran `npm run validate:projects-fixture` successfully to verify validation logic and correct error raising behavior.
2. **Manual Verification**:
   - Verified programmatically that all 226 entries in `projects.json` conform to the schema list.
   - Verified rendering on the Category Breakdown doughnut chart of the local `stats.html` server (resolved 14 distinct categories perfectly without duplicates).

items.body:
## Pull Request Description

### Related Issue
Closes #7841

### Summary
Fixed keyboard navigation support for project cards on the homepage by resolving a runtime JavaScript `ReferenceError`. 

Previously, an obsolete call to `initStreak()` was left in the `DOMContentLoaded` listener in `index.js` while its function definition had been removed in previous refactors. This caused a page-load crash that prevented the project grid from loading/rendering, meaning the project cards did not exist in the DOM and focus jumped directly from the search/filter inputs to the footer. Removing this obsolete call allows the project cards to render and receive standard focus/tab sequence correctly.

## Changes Made
- **Removed Obsolete Call:** Deleted the undefined `initStreak()` function call from the DOMContentLoaded handler in [index.js](<file:///Users/nishtha/Desktop/gssoc/100_days_100_web_project /100_days_100_web_project/index.js#L1759>).

---

## Testing and Verification

### Local Validation
- [x] Launched the local server (`npm run dev`) and loaded the homepage to verify that the console is free of JS ReferenceErrors.
- [x] Verified that project cards are rendered successfully.
- [x] Ran a local Puppeteer automated script to simulate tab sequences: focus correctly cycles from search filters into the card containers (`div.project-card`) and their inner interactive controls (`Demo`, `Code`, `Bookmark`), and then to the next card.

items.title: feat: implement database-driven daily puzzle system (#1637)
items.body:
### Description
This PR addresses issue #1637 by implementing a database-driven Daily Puzzle system. Previously, daily puzzles were statically hardcoded in a frontend array inside `board.js`, limiting the daily puzzle selection to only 7 static puzzles and requiring code redeployments to add new ones. 

This change introduces a Django model for storing puzzles, a Django admin interface for managing puzzles, and a dynamic REST API endpoint to retrieve the active puzzle. The frontend has been updated to fetch this daily puzzle dynamically.

### Key Changes
- **ChessPuzzle Django Model**: Created `ChessPuzzle` in `game/models.py` to store fields for `fen`, `solution` (JSON array), `title`, `difficulty`, and optional specific `date`.
- **Django Admin Interface**: Registered `ChessPuzzle` in a new `game/admin.py` file to enable admins to manage daily puzzles.
- **REST API Endpoint**: Added a `GET /api/puzzles/daily/` route in `game/urls.py` & `game/views.py` that serves the puzzle assigned for today's date, or falls back to a deterministic modulo choice over the database, or a default fallback if the database is empty.
- **Auto-Seeding & Migrations**: Generated schema migrations and wrote a data migration in `0017_chesspuzzle.py` that automatically seeds the 7 original frontend puzzles into the database.
- **Dynamic Frontend Fetching**: Updated `board.js` to fetch the daily puzzle asynchronously via the new endpoint on demand.
- **Unit Tests**: Added test suite `ChessPuzzleDailyApiTest` in `game/tests.py` verifying date matching, deterministic fallbacks, and empty DB handling.

### Related Issues
Closes #1637

## Description
This PR introduces the `dim_weekends` query parameter to the 3D monolith SVG generator. It allows developers to distinguish their weekend open-source contributions from their weekday contributions by applying a lower opacity (`opacity: 0.3`) to any towers representing weekend commits (Saturdays and Sundays). This highlights their weekday work schedules.

Closes #4159

---

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Types & Validation
- **`types/index.ts`**: Added optional `dim_weekends` parameter to the `BadgeParams` interface.
- **`lib/validations.ts`**: Added Zod schema validation rules mapping the `dim_weekends` query parameter to a boolean flag (using `toBooleanFlag` which accepts both `'true'` and `'1'`, defaulting to `false`).

### 2. SVG Generator & Styling
- **`lib/svg/generator.ts`**:
  - Modified the `renderTowers` function to detect weekend towers (using `t.col === 0` for Sunday and `t.col === 6` for Saturday).
  - Applied the `dimmed-tower` class and `style="opacity: 0.3;"` directly to the parent group of the weekend towers.
  - Wrapped generated floating particles of weekend towers inside a `<g class="dimmed-tower" style="opacity: 0.3;">` block to ensure their particles are also dimmed.
  - Added `.dimmed-tower { opacity: 0.3; }` to both the static and auto-theme style blocks.

### 3. Tests & Documentation
- **`docs/customization.md`**: Documented the new `dim_weekends` parameter in both parameter tables and added example links.
- **`lib/validations.test.ts`**: Added unit tests to verify the Zod validation schema behavior for `dim_weekends`.
- **`lib/svg/generator.test.ts`**: Added rendering tests ensuring that the `dimmed-tower` class, opacity style, and particle grouping are correctly applied only to weekend contribution blocks.

Closes #4157

### Description

The `InteractiveViewer` component previously only supported mouse/touch interactions for rotating and zooming the 3D monolith preview. Users navigating with a keyboard could not rotate or interact with the preview at all.

This PR adds keyboard accessibility so that arrow keys rotate the 3D camera (pitch and yaw) when in 3D mode, while keeping the existing pan behavior in non-3D mode. It also adds a descriptive `aria-label` to the container so screen readers announce the available keyboard controls.

- `+`/`-` keys continue to zoom in/out (unchanged behavior)
- Added a dynamic `aria-label` on the container element:
  - **3D mode:** *"Interactive 3D viewer. Use Arrow keys to rotate, W A S D to pan, plus and minus to zoom, R to reset."*
  - **Normal mode:** *"Interactive viewer. Use Arrow keys or W A S D to pan, plus and minus to zoom, R to reset."*
- `tabIndex={0}` and `focus-visible:ring` were already present — no changes needed

**`components/InteractiveViewer.test.tsx`**
- Added tests for arrow key rotation calling `onRotate3D` in 3D mode
- Added test confirming WASD still pans in 3D mode
- Added test confirming arrow keys fall back to panning in non-3D mode
- Added tests verifying correct `aria-label` in both modes

Automated E2E Testing Suite - **Playwright Configuration**: Added `web-app/playwright.config.js` to automatically spin up a local Python dev server (`python3 -m http.server 8000`) and test the app across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit. - **Continuous Integration (CI)**: Configured a `.github/workflows/playwright.yml` workflow to automatically execute the E2E test suite on pull requests and pushes to the main branch. - **Playwright Spec Files (`web-app/tests-e2e/`)**: - `theme.spec.js`: Validates the light/dark theme toggles, checking `data-theme` attribute changes on `` and corresponding custom CSS variable values. - `search.spec.js`: Verifies category tab filtering and search bar text inputs accurately filter the project list. - `modal.spec.js`: Confirms modal open triggers, validates modal focus traps, tests close actions (`Escape` key, close button, clicking outside), and checks that focus correctly returns to the triggering card element. - `playground.spec.js`: Exercises the CodeMirror editor playground, validates mock run actions, and checks console execution logs. ### 2. Core Web App Bug Fixes - **Modal Nesting & Focus Trap**: - Relocated the `#projectModal` element to the root level of `document.body` during initialization. This prevents it from becoming inert when the main wrappers are marked as `aria-hidden` / `inert` while the modal is open. - Resolved `ReferenceError: window.setMainInert is not a function` by correctly exposing the local function to the `window` scope. - **Sidebar Nesting & Event Propagation**: - Fixed a dangling `` in `index.html` that corrupted structural HTML layouts. - Changed the inline capture-phase click guard (`true`) on the sidebar to bubble-phase (`false`), permitting legitimate click handlers (like project card triggers) to bubble up correctly and open modals. - **Syntax & Import Errors**: - Restored the correct nesting of the main `DOMContentLoaded` listener in `js/main.js` which had been closed prematurely. - Added the missing utility import `updateProjectVisibility` to `main.js`. - **Runtime Errors**: - Updated `js/projects/tic-tac-toe.js` to only run initialization when the game's start button (`#start-btn`) exists, preventing runtime exceptions on non-game pages.

items.title: refactor: migrate PROJECTS array from brittle indexes to maintainable objects
items.body:
## Description

Fixes #4577
Currently, the projects fetched from `projects.json` are mapped into a nested array of arrays, and properties are accessed via brittle numerical indices (`project[0]`, `project[1]`, etc.) throughout the codebase. This makes modifications extremely fragile and prone to subtle bugs.

This PR refactors the `PROJECTS` array mapping in `index.js` to preserve the object keys from the JSON data. All index-based property references (including filtering, sorting, card rendering, and bookmarking systems) have been refactored to use clean, semantic object properties.

## Changes Implemented

### 1. Data Hydration & Structure
- Updated `hydrateProjects(data)` to map the fetched objects into an array of objects structured with self-documenting keys:
  ```javascript
  {
    day: `Day ${project.projectNo}`,
    projectName: project.projectName,
    projectPath: project.projectPath,
    techStack: project.techStack,
    difficulty: project.difficulty,
    projectDesc: project.projectDesc
  }

### 2. Rendering, Filters, and Sorting Refactoring
- **renderGrid**: Updated page item destructuring, grid rendering logic, card interactions, and category/search filters.
- **Sorting Logic**: Replaced numerical indexing accesses in sorting algorithms (A-Z, latest, difficulty) with direct object keys (`projectName`,` day`, `difficulty`).
- **getAllTechnologies & updateCategoryCounts**: Mapped tag lookups to utilize direct properties (`project.techStack`, `project.projectName`) instead of array elements.

### 3. Backwards Compatibility with User Bookmarks & Recents
- normalizeProjectEntry: Enhanced this helper to dynamically resolve both the legacy array-based format (project[0], project[1]) and the new object properties. This ensures that bookmarks and recently visited items saved in a user's local browser localStorage remain perfectly functional without losing data.
- Bookmarks & Recents Loops: Updated references in toggleBookmark, loadBookmarksFromURL, renderBookmarks, and renderRecentProjects to ensure objects are handled properly.

## Impact & Benefits
- Highly Readable Codebase: Eliminates cryptic numeric indexes (e.g. project[5]) in favor of self-documenting names (project.projectDesc).
- Resilient & Maintainable: Adding, removing, or re-ordering any field in projects.json will no longer require corresponding changes to dozens of lines in index.js.
- Zero Regression: Complete backwards compatibility is preserved for existing browser local storage data.

items.title: fix(a11y): announce piece captures, checks, and resignations to screen readers
items.body:
# Description

Fixes #1349 

This PR implements full screen reader compatibility for gameplay state updates, piece captures, checks, checkmates, resignations, and draws. These announcements are dispatched instantly to `a11y-announcer` in real-time, improving the accessibility experience for blind and visually impaired users.

## Key Changes

### 1. Piece Capture Announcements
- Standardized piece mappings inside `board.js` (`PIECE_NAMES`).
- When a piece is captured, the screen reader explicitly announces the event in the following format:
  - *"White captured Black's pawn on e4"*
  - *"Black captured White's queen on d5"*
- Handles capture announcements seamlessly for both manual moves (`executeMove`) and AI moves (`requestAIMove`).

### 2. Game State Announcements
- **Check**: Announces `"Check to Black King!"` or `"Check to White King!"` depending on which player is in check.
- **Checkmate**: Announces `"Checkmate. White wins!"` or `"Checkmate. Black wins!"` immediately when checkmate is delivered.
- **Resignation**: Announces `"{PlayerColor} has resigned. {WinnerColor} wins!"` (e.g. `"Black has resigned. White wins!"`).
- **Draw**: Announces `"Game drawn by agreement / stalemate / threefold repetition."` for stalemate, threefold repetition, fifty-move rule, or agreement.

### 3. Immediate Announcer Propagation
- Ensured that announcements propagate instantly to `a11y-announcer` by clearing the text and triggering a fresh accessibility update sequentially.

items.title: refactor(web): modularize main.js into focused ES6 modules (#874)
items.body:
Fixes #874 

## Description
This PR resolves issue #874 by refactoring the monolithic and highly complex `web-app/js/main.js` file (~2,100 lines) into modern, focused, and maintainable **ES6 Modules**. 

Previously, `main.js` suffered from overlapping responsibilities, duplicate/unnecessary event listeners, and duplicate helper definitions (like duplicate `prefersReducedMotion` or `debounce` declarations). 

By extracting these concerns into modular single-responsibility components and upgrading our pages to load modules natively, we have drastically improved code readability, maintainability, and load times.

---

## Architecture & Modules Introduced
A new `web-app/js/modules/` directory was created to host the following decoupled components:

1. **`utils.js`**:
   - Contains core helpers (`prefersReducedMotion`, `debounce`, `safeRun`, `escapeHtml`).
   - Introduces a new, highly optimized, and DRY helper `updateProjectVisibility(currentCategory, currentSearchQuery)` which solves search query and category filtering combinations in a single pass.
2. **`theme.js`**:
   - Manages unified site theme switches, local storage persistence, and browser metadata color-scheme updates.
   - Synchronizes the main sidebar toggle and the hero section theme toggle seamlessly.
3. **`search.js`**:
   - Handles recent search history, search input events, and dynamic dropdown autocomplete suggestion lists.
   - Adds full support for `Ctrl+K` keybindings.
   - Integrates Arrow keys navigation and Enter key selection within search suggestions.
4. **`modal.js`**:
   - Manages project modals, safe loading logic, dynamic inline instructions helper icons, and focus trapping (`trapFocus`).
   - Attaches `openProjectSafe` and `closeProjectSafe` globally to the `window` object to ensure backward compatibility for other scripts or inline HTML references.
5. **`sidebar.js`**:
   - Coordinates category selection navigation, dynamic stats counters (games, math, utilities), and mobile drawer toggle menus with safe click-outside dismissal.

---

## Entry Point and HTML Upgrades
- **`main.js`**: Rewritten as a clean and beautiful orchestrator that imports and initializes each of these modules. It handlesTimeline Serpentine SVG winding path calculations, back-to-top buttons, and sharing toast alerts.
- **Index Pages**: Upgraded `<script>` tags to `type="module"` in all page files to natively support ES6 imports:
  - `index.html`
  - `games.html`
  - `math.html`
  - `utilities.html`

items.title: fix: filter test case seeding check to current algorithm instead of global db emptiness
items.body:
Closes #478

## Bug Description
Currently, `TestCaseManager` is designed to auto-seed sample test cases (e.g., `Nearly Sorted Array` for sorting) when a user first visits a visualizer page. However, because the IndexedDB store is globally shared across all algorithms and defaults to showing all test cases (`activeTab === 'all'`), the auto-seeding logic fails for all subsequent algorithms if a user has already seeded/saved test cases for any other algorithm.

## Technical Root Cause
Inside `TestCaseManager.jsx`, the conditional check in the `load` callback checks `data.length === 0` to decide whether it should seed the current algorithm's sample cases:

```javascript
if (
  !search &&
  data.length === 0 && // <- Evaluates to false if ANY other algorithm has seeded cases
  sampleCases.length > 0 &&
  !hasSeededSamples.current
) { ... }
```

If any algorithm has already seeded or saved test cases into the database, `data.length` is greater than `0`, preventing subsequent visualizer pages from ever triggering their own seeding logic.

## Solution
Modified the check inside the `load` callback to filter the fetched test cases specifically for the current `algorithm` before determining if the database is empty:

```javascript
const currentAlgoCases = data.filter((tc) => tc.algorithm === algorithm)

if (
  !search &&
  currentAlgoCases.length === 0 && // <- Check if test cases for the CURRENT algorithm are empty
  sampleCases.length > 0 &&
  !hasSeededSamples.current
) { ... }
```

Additionally, `algorithm` was added to the `useCallback` dependency array of `load` to ensure it updates correctly when navigating between different visualizer pages.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved handling of test case loading when switching between algorithms. The application now better distinguishes between cases where the current algorithm has no test cases versus when test cases haven't been loaded at all, ensuring sample cases load appropriately based on your current algorithm selection and search filters.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

items.title: feat: accessibility and fail-safe upgrades for custom cursor ring
items.body:
# Description

This PR addresses and fixes the accessibility and fail-safe limitations of the custom cursor ring on the landing page (Issue #4999). 

## Context & Resolved Problems

- **No Fail-Safe**: Previously, if JavaScript failed to download or execute, the native system cursor remained hidden, rendering the landing page completely unusable on desktop viewports. We now keep the native system cursor visible in core CSS and only apply `cursor: none` dynamically after the custom cursor script initializes successfully.
- **Keyboard Accessibility**: The custom cursor now listens to keyboard navigation (e.g. pressing the `Tab` key) and instantly suspends itself—temporarily restoring standard system pointers and focus highlights to ensure keyboard users have a seamless browsing experience. It automatically resumes once mouse movement is detected.
- **Motion Sensitivity**: The cursor ring checks for the `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` preference dynamically. It is disabled out-of-the-box for users who have this enabled, and dynamically attaches change listeners to adapt automatically in real-time.
- **Accessibility/Theme Toggle Settings**: Added a dynamic `Cursor: Custom` / `Cursor: Default` toggle in the navigation bar (both mobile and desktop). The setting persists in `localStorage` and updates the layout immediately in real-time.

---

## Detailed Changes

### 1. Core Stylesheet (`style.css`)
- Reverted default hiding of standard pointers in base CSS to provide a robust fail-safe.
- Added a dynamic rule where `.cursor-ring` is `display: none` by default and only becomes `display: block` under `body.custom-cursor-active`.
- Applied `cursor: none !important` globally under `body.custom-cursor-active` to hide native pointers cleanly when the custom cursor is active.

### 2. Custom Cursor Lifecycle Management (`index.js`)
- Refactored the custom cursor IIFE to:
  - Check the user's persisted setting under `localStorage` (`customCursorEnabled`).
  - Check system media queries (`prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` and `pointer: coarse`).
  - Expose a global state update hook (`window.updateCustomCursorState`) to support dynamic live settings synchronization.
  - Implement a `keydown` (`Tab`) event listener to temporarily disable custom styling, restoring it on `mousemove`.
  - Set dynamic change event listeners on both media queries to handle real-time system settings shifts.

### 3. Navbar Upgrades (`navbar.js` & `index.js`)
- Integrated the new `Cursor: Custom/Default` button inside `updateNavbar()` (in `index.js`) and `initNavbar()` (in `navbar.js`) to support dynamic, multi-viewport rendering.
- Implemented a global click handler on `#cursorToggleNav` to toggle states, update button labels/icons instantly, persist settings, and trigger state updates dynamically.

---

Fixes #4999

items.title: Feat/expand tests
items.body:
# Description

This Pull Request expands the Pytest automated unit test suite to improve coverage of mathematical and utility scripts as described in #788. It introduces dedicated unit test files for four core components, and refactors `Bubble-Sort.py` into helper functions to allow clean importing and headless automated testing.

Fixes #788

## Proposed Changes

### Core Refactoring
- **`math/Bubble-Sort/Bubble-Sort.py`**: Refactored to extract the core sorting logic into a reusable `bubble_sort(arr: list[int], reverse: bool = False)` function and wrapped the CLI interactive prompt inside a `main()` function protected by the standard `if __name__ == "__main__":` block. This prevents interactive loops from executing upon test imports.

### New Test Suites
- **`tests/test_bubble_sort.py`**:
  - Tests ascending and descending sort order algorithms.
  - Tests edge cases such as empty lists, single-item lists, already sorted lists, and lists containing negative integers.
  - Validates `main()` CLI flow (including error handling for non-integer input and blank fields) using input mocking.
- **`tests/test_pascal_triangle.py`**:
  - Validates row generations for various values of `n` (e.g., `n=1`, `n=5`).
  - Ensures edge cases (`n <= 0`) return empty lists correctly.
  - Tests both display modes (full triangle vs. specific row selection) and invalid choices/bounds checking.
- **`tests/test_happy_number.py`**:
  - Tests known happy numbers (e.g., `19`, `7`) and unhappy numbers (e.g., `4`), ensuring returned calculation sequences are completely correct.
  - Mocks out the Tkinter-based `run_visualizer` function to prevent test suites from launching visual UI windows and hanging in headless CI/CD environments.
- **`tests/test_caesar_cipher.py`**:
  - Validates shifting strings forwards (encryption) and backwards (decryption) preserving letter casing.
  - Verifies spaces, numbers, and punctuation are ignored and left untouched.
  - Tests CLI operation routing and handling of non-integer shift value inputs.

items.body:
### Description

This Pull Request addresses and resolves #1285 by implementing a robust, premium client-side multi-premove queue with visual directional trails and dashed blue outlines. 

Instead of a simple single pre-move system, this enhancement enables players to plan and queue multiple consecutive moves during the AI's turn.

### Key Features Implemented

1. **Multi-Premove Queue**: 
   - Transformed `premove` from a single object state to an array queue: `let premoveQueue = []`.
   - Enabled queuing consecutive moves on the board during the AI's/opponent's turn.

2. **Virtual Board State Representation**:
   - Added a `getVirtualBoard()` simulation method. This dynamically constructs a virtual representation of the board as it would appear after all currently queued pre-moves are executed.
   - Integrated `getVirtualBoard()` with piece selection, dragging, and touch events (including mobile touch drag-and-drop), enabling players to select and queue consecutive moves with the same piece or pieces that have already been virtually moved.

3. **Sequential Execution & Robust Cancellation**:
   - Programmed the sequential execution of pre-moves once the opponent finishes their turn.
   - Implemented check validation: if the first queued pre-move becomes illegal (due to the opponent's move capturing the piece or rendering the path invalid), the current move and all subsequent queued pre-moves are cancelled.
   - Cleared the queue instantly upon validation errors, loading a new game state, selecting a new game, or right-clicking on the board.

4. **Responsive Visual Paths**:
   - Designed a dynamic and premium SVG-based canvas overlay (`#premove-svg-overlay`) that renders vibrant blue arrows indicating the exact direction and sequence of queued moves.
   - Listened to the window `resize` event to automatically redraw the SVG overlay keeping trails aligned with the chessboard's dynamic sizing.

5. **Premove Visual Style**:
   - Replaced old red background highlights on pre-moved squares with a premium CSS dashed outline: `2px dashed #3b82f6` with a subtle semi-transparent blue backdrop.

items.title: `fix: replace iframe symptom rendering with streamlit markdown in dashboard`
items.body:
Closes #342

## Description
This pull request replaces the resource-heavy and sluggish iframe sandbox-based symptom selection and search highlighting in the Patient Symptom Analysis section of `dashboard.py` with native Streamlit components and HTML-highlighted text.

### Problem
Previously, the app rendered a custom `components.html()` sandbox iframe for every single symptom checkbox and search suggestion in the symptom list loop. Spawning dozens of asynchronous iframe sandboxes in the browser led to:
- **High Memory Usage**: Significant client-side memory footprint.
- **Interactivity Lag**: Sluggish scrolling and delay during search and selection.
- **Layout Instability (CLS)**: Jumpy interface behavior during dynamic iframe height calculations.
- **Broken Merge Layout**: A previous merge conflict resolution had also duplicated loops and introduced syntax/scoping errors in this section.

### Solution
- **Lightweight Highlighting**: Added a text-highlighting helper utility `highlight_text` using regex `re.sub` to wrap matching queries in standard HTML `<mark>` tags.
- **Native Render Elements**: Replaced `components.html()` with native Streamlit `st.markdown(..., unsafe_allow_html=True)` inside the search suggestion and symptom lists, preventing iframe generation.
- **Clean Alignment**: Redesigned the 2-column symptom grid using `st.columns([1, 10])` to align the standard selection checkbox and the highlighted markdown text side-by-side cleanly.
- **Zero-Dependency Refactor**: Completely removed the `streamlit.components.v1` import as it is no longer required.

## Benefits
- **Dramatic Performance Boost**: Symptoms and suggestions now load instantly without asynchronous iframe rendering overhead.
- **Zero Layout Shift (CLS)**: The layout is highly stable with standard native text sizes and structure.
- **Ultra-low Client-Side Memory**: Highlighting is executed in standard DOM nodes rather than isolated browser sandboxes.

## Changes Made
- [x] Imported `re` and defined `highlight_text` helper function in `dashboard.py`
- [x] Added dynamic symptom search input and suggestions list using `st.markdown` with inline HTML support
- [x] Refactored the symptom grid to align checkboxes and highlighted text using `st.columns([1, 10])` side-by-side natively
- [x] Removed unused `streamlit.components.v1` import and references

items.title: Refactor RateLimiter to support pluggable backends and automatic cleanup
items.body:
Closes #340

## Description
This PR refactors the `RateLimiter` class in `backend/middleware/security.py` to support pluggable, concurrent-safe storage backends (In-Memory, Redis, and SQLite) and solves memory leak/unbounded growth concerns with automatic, periodic background pruning.

### Key Changes
1. **Pluggable Architecture for Rate Limiting**:
   - Refactored `RateLimiter` to decouple storage logic into three distinct backend options under `backend/middleware/security.py`:
     - **`InMemoryBackend`**: Thread-safe implementation using Python's `defaultdict` wrapped in a `threading.Lock`. Features an automatic background pruning daemon thread (`threading.Thread`) that periodically removes old records (older than 5 minutes) and deletes empty keys to prevent memory leaks/unbounded growth.
     - **`RedisBackend`**: Designed for distributed environments (such as multiple Gunicorn worker processes). Uses an atomic Lua script for checking and recording requests within the sliding window, leveraging Redis TTL keys (`EXPIRE`) for native, automatic cleanup.
     - **`SQLiteBackend`**: A shared cache backend utilizing a local SQLite database file configured in Write-Ahead Logging (WAL) mode for concurrency support among isolated OS worker processes, with automatic periodic cleanups.
   - Configured `RateLimiter` to read `RATE_LIMIT_BACKEND` from `.env`, dynamically resolving the chosen storage, and falling back gracefully to thread-safe `InMemoryBackend` if Redis is requested but cannot be reached or the package is missing.

2. **Dependencies**:
   - Added `redis>=5.0.0` inside `requirements.txt`.

3. **Syntax / Indentation Repair**:
   - Repaired an indentation syntax error (unexpected spacing) on lines 102–113 of `backend/tests/test_validation.py` to restore standard test suite discovery and execution.

4. **Comprehensive Unit Testing**:
   - Created a brand-new, robust unit test suite in `backend/tests/test_rate_limiter.py` verifying:
     - Rolling-window request allowance and correct remaining request bounds.
     - Blocked limits and precise `retry_after` timing.
     - In-memory auto-pruning / cleanup.
     - SQLite file-based WAL rate limiting and table pruning.
     - Mocked distributed Redis backend with atomic Lua script execution.
     - Graceful fallbacks and dynamic configuration matching.

items.title: fix(layout): resolve algorithm cards becoming hidden after page refresh
items.body:
## Fixes Issue: #348

## Summary
On page refresh/reload, several algorithm cards on the home page were cut off, partially hidden, or completely invisible. This was caused by two main issues:
1. **Vertical Layout Clipping**: The parent wrappers (`AppLayout` and `Home` page wrapper) utilized `overflow-hidden` which translates to `overflow: hidden`. During scroll restoration or layout updates on refresh, this would truncate vertical content.
2. **Animation In-View Threshold Misconfiguration**: The cards grid container utilized Framer Motion's `whileInView` with a strict negative viewport margin of `margin: '-100px'`. This required cards to be deep inside the viewport to trigger their staggered fade-in/slide-up animations. On small viewports or when scroll positions were restored, cards would remain stuck in their initial hidden state (`opacity: 0, y: 30`).

This PR resolves these layout bugs by switching the overflow strategy to vertical-safe `overflow-x-hidden` and adjusting the Framer Motion viewport intersection amount.

---

## Proposed Changes

### 1. App Layout Component (`src/components/AppLayout.jsx`)
- Replaced `overflow-hidden` with `overflow-x-hidden` on the main `<motion.div>` wrapper. This enables vertical layout boundaries to resize naturally while still blocking horizontal layout overflows.

### 2. Algorithms Grid Container (`src/components/Home.jsx`)
- Replaced `overflow-hidden` with `overflow-x-hidden` on the outermost `<div className="theme-home">` container.
- Updated the Framer Motion `<motion.div>` grid wrapper element's viewport configuration to use `amount: 0.1` instead of `margin: '-100px'`. This ensures that cards stagger-animate into view smoothly as soon as 10% of the grid enters the viewport on all screen heights.

items.title: Fix: chessboard border alignment
items.body:
### Description

This PR fixes the chessboard border alignment issue where the board squares did not align correctly with the outer border container on all four sides (leaving uneven padding/spacing). 

### Root Cause
The `.chessboard` grid container was explicitly styled with `box-sizing: content-box` while its width and height were calculated to include the board border:
`width: calc(var(--square-size) * 8 + var(--board-border) * 2)`

Under `content-box`, the border width is added outside the calculated dimensions. This left an internal gap of `var(--board-border) * 2` (6px) in the content area around the grid squares, causing visual misalignment and uneven gaps at the edges of the board.

### Solution
Updated `.chessboard`'s box-sizing to `border-box` in `board.css`. This ensures:
* The borders are correctly included within the specified width and height.
* The content area exactly matches `var(--square-size) * 8`, allowing the 8x8 grid of squares to fit flush against the borders on all four sides.
* Alignment is correct across all themes, desktop resolutions, and responsive mobile layouts (where square sizes are dynamically calculated).

### Related Issues
Closes #1066

### Checklist
- [x] Sizing issues checked and verified
- [x] Consistent box-model styling applied
- [x] Alignment verified on both desktop and mobile viewports

## Summary

This PR addresses the issue requesting an automated testing system. It sets up a unified `pytest`-based test suite covering core helper functions across multiple mini-projects, and configures a GitHub Actions workflow to run tests automatically on every Pull Request.

---

## Changes Made

| File | What's Tested | Tests |
|---|---|---|

items.title: feat: sort project cards lexicographically for better navigation
items.body:
### Description
Fixes #603 

This PR resolves the issue where project cards on the homepage were displayed in an inconsistent/random order. It ensures that all project cards are sorted lexicographically (alphabetically) by their title for better navigation and UX.

### Proposed Changes
- **Dynamic Sorting in `main.js`**: Reordered the project card DOM elements inside `.projects-grid` alphabetically using `localeCompare` on their `<h3>` text content right at the start of the `DOMContentLoaded` event handler.
- **Downstream Safety**: Since the sorting runs before defining references like `projectCards`, all downstream interactions (filtering, search, favorites, category tabs) automatically operate on the sorted elements.
- **Automatic & Scalable**: Avoids manually rearranging thousands of lines of static HTML files, and ensures any future cards added to the HTML will automatically be sorted lexicographically without developer intervention.

### Benefits
- **Zero layout shift**: Elements are reordered synchronously on `DOMContentLoaded` before rendering is complete.
- **Consistent sorting**: Works consistently across the homepage (`index.html`) as well as all specific categories (`games.html`, `math.html`, `utilities.html`).

### Verification & Testing
- Ran all local Python unit tests via `pytest` to ensure zero regressions to the calculation modules:
  `119 passed in 0.69s`
- Verified visual correctness: project cards are properly sorted starting from `AP/GP/AGP/HP Recognizer`, `Armstrong Numbers` through to `Word Scramble` / `Whack-a-Mole`.

items.title: fix(playground): resolve layout distortion and hero elements overlap
items.body:
## Description
This PR resolves the layout distortion and hero element overlap issue in the Interactive Python Playground section on the homepage when the Playground category tab is active. (Closes #509)

---

## The Problem
When switching to the Playground category:

- The projects list becomes hidden, reducing the height of the main content wrapper.
- `.playground-section` lacked:
  - its own stacking context
  - relative positioning
  - a solid background

Because of this, decorative elements from `.hero-section` (background grids, floating code snippets, canvas elements, etc.) overlapped into the playground editor and console output area, making the UI distorted and partially unreadable.

---

## The Solution
Added the following styles to `.playground-section` in `web-app/index.html`:

```css
position: relative;
z-index: 2;
background-color: var(--bg-color);
```

These changes ensure:
- proper stacking behavior
- hero decorations stay behind the playground
- editor and console render correctly without overlap

items.title: fix: resolve platform-specific winsound startup crash on macOS/Linux
items.body:
### 📝 Description

This PR resolves a platform-specific startup crash in the **Math-Quiz** game when launched on non-Windows platforms (macOS/Linux) and implements a native Tkinter sound fallback.

#### 🔍 Cause
The Windows-only standard library module `winsound` was unconditionally imported at the module level in `games/Math-Quiz/Math-Quiz.py`. On macOS and Linux, this raised a fatal `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'winsound'` immediately upon launch.

#### 🛠️ Solution
1. **Graceful Platform Check**: Wrapped the module-level import of `winsound` in a robust `try...except ImportError` block, defaulting to `winsound = None` if the module is not supported.
2. **Native Tkinter Sound Fallback**: Updated the `play_sound` utility function to safely check for the availability of `winsound`. If unavailable, it leverages Tkinter's native `tk._default_root.bell()` method.
3. **Consolidated Backups**: Safely falls back to triggering the terminal bell (`\a`) only if a Tkinter root window is not instantiated/available, fully preventing stdout pollution.

### 🔗 Linked Issue
Closes #391

---

items.title: fix: Resolve non-functional category filter buttons
items.body:
Closes #314

## 🐛 Bug
The category filter buttons on the homepage (`All Projects`, `Games`, `Math`, `Utilities`, `Playground`) were completely non-functional. Clicking any category produced no response because JavaScript execution stopped due to syntax errors in `web-app/js/main.js`.

## 🔍 Root Cause
The issue was caused by multiple merge-conflict related problems inside `main.js`:

- Duplicate declaration of `savedTheme` (`const` and `var` in the same scope)
- Orphaned duplicate code blocks referencing undeclared variables
- Missing `DOMContentLoaded` wrapper causing scope issues
- Broken `renderRecentSearches()` function with missing closing braces
- Undefined `syncThemeColor()` function being called multiple times
- Event handlers placed outside proper execution scope

Because the script failed during parsing, all subsequent interactive features stopped working, including:
- Category filtering
- Search functionality
- Theme toggle
- Modals
- Card click interactions
- Random project feature

## ✅ Fix Implemented
Refactored and stabilized `main.js` without changing existing functionality:

- Removed duplicate/orphaned conflicting code
- Restored proper `DOMContentLoaded` structure
- Fixed missing closing braces and broken function blocks
- Added missing `syncThemeColor()` implementation
- Reorganized event listeners into correct scope
- Preserved all existing UI behavior and logic

## 🧪 Result
All homepage category filter buttons now work correctly and JavaScript functionality has been fully restored across the application.
